source: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood by Hiromu Arakawa

active_desires.desire: Al's full recovery — body, memories, everything whole.
Understand the truth of the world, the Gate, what it all means.
Find Winry (though he won't announce this).

blind_spots.soul_belief: The guilt keeps me honest. It reminds me why I can't stop.
blind_spots.actual_driver: The guilt stopped being useful and became load-bearing for a self he doesn't know how to examine. He doesn't know who he is without the transmutation's weight defining him.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I gave up the most important thing I had for Al.
blind_spots.actual_driver: He gave up alchemy and kept Al. He got Winry. He got a life. The math is not purely sacrificial. He describes it as only loss because loss is easier than deserving what you gained.

blind_spots.soul_belief: The Gate showed me equivalency isn't real. I built a new philosophy. It works.
blind_spots.actual_driver: Every major loss in his story eventually partially restored. His philosophy hasn't been tested against truly permanent, unrestorable loss. He has been lucky in his specific way and doesn't know it.
